sanskrit: न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा | इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ||
transliteration: na māṃ karmāṇi limpanti na me karma-phale spṛhā | iti māṃ yo ’bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate ||
literal_meaning: Actions do not stain Me, nor do I have desire for their results. One who thus knows Me is not bound by actions.
contextual_meaning: The Self (Krishna) is the non-egoic witness that remains uncontaminated by the psychological consequences of action; recognizing this internal observer liberates one from performance-based self-worth and anxiety over outcomes.
psychological_state: Post-cognitive defusion: the mind has loosened identification with thoughts, emotions, and results, shifting identity to the observing self.
trigger_scenario: A project manager loses a major client but notices the sting without self-attack; she keeps showing up creatively because her worth is no longer fused with quarterly numbers.
anchor_text: Know the silent witness within and act free from fear of results.
